page: Amazon Web Services m8gb.2xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 8 dedicated physical cores on the ARM64 architecture, utilizing the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.8 GHz. The processor is equipped with 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache. It is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. Local storage is not bundled with this instance, and it lacks dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 17 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong memory subsystem capabilities, with the PassMark memory score reaching the top 10% tier. Memory bandwidth tests show read-only speeds of 924.98 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 153.8 GB/sec for larger uncached blocks, alongside a write speed of 86.29 GB/sec. Single-core performance is notable, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores falling into the top 25% tier. However, the instance exhibits weaker performance in cryptographic operations, with the PassMark CPU encryption test scoring in the bottom 25%. Large-scale LLM inference is also a bottleneck, as prompt processing for a medium 7B model ranks in the bottom 10% tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m8gb.2xlarge leverages custom ARM64 silicon to deliver competitive resource density and high memory performance without the overhead of x86 licensing. This makes it a viable option for workloads that benefit from high memory bandwidth and steady-state single-threaded performance. The instance is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, Redis key-value stores, database operations, and text processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated ARM64 cores powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a 17 Gbps baseline network speed. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data highlights top-tier memory performance, with PassMark memory scores in the top 10% and strong single-core Geekbench results. However, it shows weaker performance in cryptographic encryption and medium-model LLM prompt processing. The m8gb.2xlarge is suited for general-purpose applications, database operations, static web serving, and memory-intensive caching workloads.

tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance delivering top-tier memory performance and balanced compute resources for database and web-serving workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 at 2.8 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory with top-tier PassMark memory benchmark performance
17 Gbps baseline network bandwidth utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Weaker performance in CPU encryption and medium-model LLM prompt processing
Suited for database operations, Redis caching, and static web serving
